race.name: Lemming Loppet
race.tagline: Germany's Black Forest answer to Sweden's Vasaloppet — a newer marathon through one of Central Europe's most atmospheric Nordic landscapes.

climate.description: Snow conditions in the Black Forest are increasingly variable. Temperatures typically -5°C to 3°C. Snowmaking may supplement natural snow.
challenges: Unreliable snow conditions due to climate change
Mild winters can force cancellation or course modification
Fog common in Black Forest valleys

course.primary: Trail course through the Northern Black Forest with typical Central European rolling terrain through dense fir forest.

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: A newer event still finding its footing. Snow reliability in the Black Forest is the main challenge.

history.summary: Founded in 2016, the Lemming Loppet is a newer Nordic ski marathon modeled after Sweden's Vasaloppet. Set in Germany's Black Forest, it brings the Scandinavian loppet tradition to Central Europe.
notable_facts: Founded 2016 — one of Germany's newest ski marathons
Black Forest has a long Nordic skiing tradition

youtube_data.search_query: Lemming Loppet cross country ski race Northern Black Forest
